title: Delicate Crumbly Korean Rice Flour Jeon
description: A traditional Korean dish featuring crumbly rice flour pancakes, this jeon is a vegetarian friendly and umami rich treat, often served in a hot pot vessel and paired with tempering chocolate for a unique flavor combination.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour, all purpose flour, salt, sugar, and black pepper.
Gradually add in water and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Heat the vegetable oil in a hot pot vessel over medium heat.
Using a ladle, drop small amounts of the batter into the oil and cook until the bottom is golden brown.
Loosen the jeon with a spatula and flip it over to cook the other side.
Once cooked, remove the jeon from the oil and place it on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the jeon hot with melted tempering chocolate, chopped kimchi, and green onions.
Repeat the cooking process with the remaining batter.
